Financial Performance

Step-by-step savings calculation from historical benchmark to earned savings/losses.

Step Description Value MSSP Median Peer Median
1 Assigned Beneficiaries 15,819 p58 13,159 11,432
2 Historical Benchmark (per capita) $12,314 p70 $11,439 $11,024
3 Regional Adjustment +$292
4 Prior Savings Adjustment +-
5 Adjustment Category Regional Adjustment
6 Updated Benchmark (per capita) $14,423 p73 $13,279 $12,889
7 Total Benchmark (all beneficiaries) $223,769,250
8 Total Expenditure (all beneficiaries) $200,868,304
9 Per Capita Expenditure (PY) $12,947 p56 $12,693 $12,248
10 Generated Savings / Losses $22,900,946 $6,931,306 —
11 Savings Rate 10.23% p88 4.21% 4.83%
12 Minimum Savings Rate (threshold) 2.67%
13 Guardrail Applied —
14 Final Shared Savings Rate 40.00%
15 Final Shared Loss Rate -
16 Earned Savings / Losses $8,977,171 $3,685,876 —

Per Capita Expenditure by Population Segment

Annual per-beneficiary expenditure across benchmark years (BY1–BY3) and the performance year (PY). Includes Parts A and B FFS spending.

Population Segment BY1BY2BY3PY Trend Risk-Adj. Expected Difference
End-Stage Renal Disease (ESRD) $93,727 $92,554 $96,783 $101,678 ↑ $88,821 +$12,857

Aged, Non-Dual $9,871 $10,296 $10,781 $12,443 ↑ $11,902 +$541
Total $12,947 $12,693 $12,248

Risk Scores

Hierarchical Condition Category risk scores. A score of 1.0 = national average. Below 1.0 means the population is healthier than average.

CMS-HCC Risk Scores

Population Segment BY1BY2BY3PY Trend Coding Gap MSSP Med.
End-Stage Renal Disease (ESRD) 1.078 1.067 0.984 0.951 ↓ -0.047 0.967
Disabled 1.061 1.020 1.020 0.971 ↓ -0.018 0.934
Aged, Dual-Eligible 1.058 1.022 0.987 0.963 ↓ -0.044 0.954
Aged, Non-Dual 1.029 1.008 0.992 0.987 ↓ -0.004 0.983

Demographic Risk Scores

Demographic-only risk scores (age/sex/Medicaid status). Compared with HCC risk scores, the difference reflects clinical coding intensity.

Population Segment BY3PY
End-Stage Renal Disease (ESRD) — 0.998
Disabled — 0.989
Aged, Dual-Eligible — 1.007
Aged, Non-Dual — 0.991

Risk Redistribution Weights (PY)

Fraction of the ACO's total risk attributable to each population segment.

ESRD Weight 1.9%
Disabled Weight 10.1%
Aged Dual Weight 7.6%
Aged Non-Dual Weight 80.4%

MSSP quality measures. Measures marked "lower is better" are inverted — a lower score indicates better performance.

Utilization — Admissions & Visits Psychiatric Admissions (per 1,000) * 3 ADM_Psych
Utilization — Admissions & Visits Rehab Admissions (per 1,000) 9 15 ADM_Rehab
Utilization — Admissions & Visits Short-Term Acute Admissions (per 1,000) 236 229 ADM_S_Trm
Utilization — Admissions & Visits SNF Admissions (per 1,000) 42 40 P_SNF_ADM
Utilization — Admissions & Visits SNF Average Length of Stay (days) 29 27 p68 SNF_LOS
Utilization — Admissions & Visits SNF Average Payment per Stay $17,511 $15,338 p72 SNF_PayperStay
Methodology

Data source: MSSP ACO Performance PY2024 (dataset ID: mssp-performance). Vintage: PY2024. Downloaded: 2026-04-12. Rows in source: 476.

MSSP ACO Performance reports financial and quality performance for Accountable Care Organizations participating in the Medicare Shared Savings Program. Savings/losses are calculated against risk-adjusted, regionally-weighted benchmarks.

Full methodology →

MIT License. Free. No ads. No login.
